desc: Riga combines standout Art Nouveau boulevards, a lively old town, and a compact riverfront layout with strong Baltic café/bar culture. It is most comfortable from late spring through early autumn, while December offers festive atmosphere and short-day winter city-break appeal.
hls: Old Town lanes with strong café/bar culture
Huge Central Market in historic hangars
Fast access to Jūrmala beach and pine coast

todo.name: Old Town + Daugava embankment
todo.desc: Walk cathedral squares and riverside viewpoints to orient yourself in the medieval core.

todo.name: Alberta iela architecture route
todo.desc: Dedicate time to the Art Nouveau quarter and decorative facades around the Quiet Centre.

todo.name: Central Market tasting pass
todo.desc: Sample smoked fish, rye breads, pickles, and Latvian snacks inside the old zeppelin hangars.

todo.name: National Library sunset view
todo.desc: Cross the river for skyline views; in winter, plan this early because daylight ends quickly.

todo.name: Jūrmala half-day
todo.desc: Take the train for sea air and pine walks, especially rewarding from late spring to early autumn.

todo.name: Winter sauna + warm bars
todo.desc: In dark-season months, combine spa/sauna sessions with cozy cocktail or beer venues.

wifi.note: Very strong in hotels, apartments, and city cafés, with wide eSIM/public-Wi-Fi availability.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick-Riga is commonly available nonstop with strong frequency in peak periods. Return fares are often ~£35-£95 off-peak, ~£95-£170 in shoulder demand, and ~£170+ around midsummer and December festive peaks.
prac.lang: Latvian (Russian widely understood in some settings)
prac.visa: Latvia is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: From late November to January, daylight is short; plan outdoor sightseeing around a 6-7 hour light window.
Cruise arrivals can create temporary midday crowding in old-town lanes during summer.
Winter pavements may be icy — choose footwear suitable for freezing conditions.

sweet: June-August usually offers the longest daylight and best weather odds, while September often balances value and comfort well.
